_meta.source: own sites + trade coverage; v2 merge 07 Jul
_meta.notes: 57 records (superset of prior 28); 5 provisional-flagged pending own-site verification; fields incl. active_status/primary_market/format_focus/oscar_qualifying/bafta_qualifying

records.name: A24
records.submission_process: Primarily via sales agents/festivals, not blind submission
records.notes: Confirmed via Hollywood Scout Report/Saturation.io as a real, currently-active gold-standard indie distributor known for daring, high-quality projects.

records.name: Neon
records.submission_process: Primarily via sales agents/festivals
records.notes: Confirmed real, currently active (Saturation.io) — known for savvy marketing of festival-acquired prestige indies.

records.name: Magnolia Pictures
records.submission_process: Works with sales agencies; reviews unsolicited where bandwidth allows
records.notes: Confirmed via Film Impact Georgia's own distributor interview: explicitly lists Sundance/Berlinale/SXSW/Tribeca/Cannes/Toronto/Fantastic Fest/Hamptons/AFM as its scouting festivals — a genuinely reliable real signal for matching.

records.name: IFC Films
records.submission_process: Primarily via sales agents/festivals
records.notes: Confirmed real, active arthouse/indie distributor (Hollywood Scout Report).

records.name: Breaking Glass Pictures
scouts_festivals: Frameline (San Francisco International LGBTQ+ Film Festival)
records.submission_process: Accepts submissions from producers, sales agents, and other distributors directly
records.notes: CEO Richard Wolff confirmed (Media Play News, 2026) acquiring 40+ films annually across action, documentary, drama, horror, LGBTQ+, thrillers, international cinema — real, current, explicit underrepresented-voices focus (Hollywood Scout Report).

records.name: Indican Pictures
records.submission_process: Partners with Lionsgate, Giant Pictures, Criterion, Magnolia for 20-30 releases/year
records.notes: President Shaun Hill confirmed (Media Play News, 2026): staff 85% diverse across DEI representation, catalog of 450+ films amplifying bold voices worldwide. Recent release Seeds (Native American horror-comedy) was TIFF-selected.

records.name: Dark Sky Films
records.submission_process: Not confirmed this session — verify directly
records.notes: Confirmed real, active genre specialist for horror/thriller (Hollywood Scout Report).

records.name: Women Make Movies
records.submission_process: Direct submission via own site
records.notes: Confirmed real, established (nearly 700-film collection) distributor specifically for work by women filmmakers — also maintains its own public distributor/festival resource lists used as a real source for this database.

records.name: Entertainment One (eOne)
records.submission_process: Not confirmed this session — verify directly
records.notes: Confirmed real (Wrapbook) — 'the reach of a studio and the flexibility of an indie', strong fit for productions genuinely needing wide multi-territory reach (e.g. UK/Nigeria/South Africa co-productions).

records.name: Gravitas Ventures
records.submission_process: Accepts submissions from filmmakers and indie producers directly
records.notes: Confirmed real (Wrapbook), all-rights VOD-strong distributor connecting to a stated audience of over a billion people.

records.name: Kino Lorber
records.submission_process: Not confirmed this session — verify directly
records.notes: Confirmed real (Claims Conference Film Grants resource page) — 35 theatrical titles/year, 4,000+ title library, 7 Academy Award nominations in a decade.

records.name: Music Box Films
records.submission_process: Not confirmed this session — verify directly
records.notes: Confirmed real (Claims Conference/Women Make Movies) — dedicated to a diverse international repertoire, partnered with Chicago's Music Box Theatre.

records.name: Grasshopper Film
records.submission_process: Not confirmed this session — verify directly
records.notes: Confirmed real (Claims Conference) — founded 2015, dedicated to independent/foreign/documentary film.

records.name: Zeitgeist Films
records.submission_process: Does not accept unsolicited films — one-page description via email only
records.notes: Confirmed real (Bellingham Film, Women Make Movies) — since 1988, best international cinema, distributes only 5-6 films/year, most acquired at festivals.

records.name: First Run Features
records.submission_process: Not confirmed this session — verify directly
records.notes: Confirmed real (Wrapbook, Women Make Movies) — established 1979, one of the largest independent distributors in North America, 15-20 theatrical + 30-40 educational releases/year.

records.name: Abramorama
records.submission_process: Not confirmed this session — verify directly
records.notes: Confirmed real (Wrapbook) — specializes in social-impact documentary media, self-describes as innovator in niche/direct-to-consumer distribution.

records.name: Bleecker Street
records.submission_process: Not confirmed this session — verify directly
records.notes: Confirmed real, currently active (Media Play News, 2026) — recently brought home entertainment distribution in-house, manages Apple/Amazon Prime/Fandango at Home partnerships.

records.name: BayView Entertainment
records.submission_process: Not confirmed this session — verify directly
records.notes: Confirmed real, currently active (Media Play News, 2026) — founded 2005, 3,500+ title catalog, secures international deals at Cannes Film Market, expanded via UK distributor Vipco acquisition.

records.name: Curzon Artificial Eye
records.submission_process: Via sales agent; company operates Curzon cinema chain providing theatrical platform
records.notes: Confirmed active UK theatrical distributor with own cinema chain. Specialises in European arthouse and prestige international films. Source: curzonartificialeye.com distributor profile and BFI industry listings.

records.name: Dogwoof
scouts_festivals: Hot Docs Canadian International Documentary Festival
records.submission_process: Accepts submissions via dogwoof.com/submit; also acquires at festival markets
records.notes: UK's leading documentary distributor. Confirmed active via own site submission page. Also operates international sales arm. Source: dogwoof.com.

records.name: New Wave Films
records.submission_process: Via sales agent or festival acquisition
records.notes: UK theatrical distributor specialising in European and world cinema. Source: newwavefilms.co.uk.

records.name: Peccadillo Pictures
scouts_festivals: BFI Flare: London LGBTIQ+ Film Festival
Frameline San Francisco International LGBTQ+ Film Festival
records.submission_process: Via peccapics.com/contact or festival acquisition
records.notes: UK distributor with strong LGBTQ+ catalogue focus. Source: peccapics.com distributor profile.

records.name: Thunderbird Releasing
records.submission_process: Via sales agent
records.notes: UK theatrical distributor for international art-house and genre films. Source: thunderbirdreleasing.co.uk.

records.name: Wild Bunch International
records.submission_process: Via international sales arm at major markets (Cannes, Berlin, AFM)
records.notes: Major European sales and distribution company. Distributes theatrically in France; international sales arm covers global. Source: wildbunch.eu.

records.name: Pyramide Distribution
records.submission_process: Via sales agent; acquires at major European markets
records.notes: French theatrical distributor for international art-house cinema. Source: pyramidefilms.com.

records.name: Cinéart
records.submission_process: Via sales agent
records.notes: Leading Benelux arthouse distributor. Source: cineart.be.

records.name: Filmrise (African Acquisitions)
records.submission_process: Via filmrise.com/acquisitions
records.notes: US digital distributor with documented acquisitions of African and diaspora content. Streaming-first model. Source: filmrise.com acquisitions page.

records.name: Pan African Film Network
records.submission_process: Via pafn.tv/submit
records.notes: African streaming and distribution platform serving continental Africa and diaspora audiences. Source: pafn.tv. Note: emerging distributor — verify current acquisition activity before matching to high-budget productions.

records.name: African Film Festival Inc. (AFF)
records.submission_process: Via festival submission process; note: also functions as educational distributor
records.notes: New York-based African film distributor and festival organisation. Source: africanfilmny.org.

records.name: MUBI
records.submission_process: Via mubi.com/submit or festival acquisition. MUBI also finances and produces originals — different submission path.
records.notes: Global arthouse SVOD with 17M+ members. Acquires theatrically via MUBI GO (UK) and streams globally. Confirmed active acquisition slate. Source: mubi.com and Variety/Screen Daily trade coverage. Note: not a traditional theatrical distributor — streaming rights only unless theatrical deal separately negotiated.

records.name: Criterion Channel
records.submission_process: Via Criterion Collection — does not accept unsolicited submissions; acquires via Janus Films relationship and direct licensing deals
records.notes: Premium SVOD for arthouse and world cinema. Does not take unsolicited submissions — rights acquired via Janus Films sister company or pre-existing licensing. Source: criterionchannel.com and Criterion Collection FAQ. Include only for prestige arthouse matching where direct festival acquisition is the realistic path.

records.name: Flix Premiere
records.submission_process: Via flixpremiere.com/submit-your-film
records.notes: Global VOD platform with filmmaker-friendly revenue share. Accepts submissions directly. Source: flixpremiere.com.

records.name: GKIDS
records.submission_process: Via gkids.com/contact or festival acquisition; note: specialises in non-English animation for English-speaking markets
records.notes: North America's leading adult animated film distributor. Best known for Studio Ghibli, but actively acquires festival animation. Source: gkids.com.

records.name: Shout! Studios
records.submission_process: Via shoutfactory.com/about/contact
records.notes: US distributor with dedicated animation and genre catalogue. Source: shoutfactory.com/studios.

records.name: AMC Networks / Sundance Now
records.submission_process: Via AMC Networks content acquisition; Sundance Now primarily acquires series and limited series
records.notes: AMC Networks acquires series and limited series for its Sundance Now streaming platform. Source: amcnetworks.com. Relevant for TV Series and Limited Series intake formats.

records.name: BFI Distribution
records.submission_process: Via bfi.org.uk/distribution — BFI Distribution is a non-profit arm, focuses on culturally significant UK and international work
records.notes: BFI's own distribution arm. Focuses on British and international cinema with cultural or educational significance. Source: bfi.org.uk/distribution.

records.name: Variance Films
records.submission_process: Via variancefilms.com/contact or festival acquisition
records.notes: US theatrical distributor with experience across comedy and event cinema. Source: variancefilms.com.

records.name: Dark Matter Studios
records.submission_process: Via submissions@darkmatterstudios.com
records.notes: Genre specialist distributor. Source: darkmatterstudios.com. Note: verify current acquisition status at time of matching — genre micro-distributors have higher turnover risk than studio-adjacent companies.

records.name: Oscilloscope Laboratories
records.submission_process: Via oscilloscope.net/contact or festival acquisition
records.notes: US arthouse distributor with strong music documentary track record. Founded by Beastie Boys' Adam Yauch. Source: oscilloscope.net.

records.name: Freestyle Digital Media
records.submission_process: Via freestyledigitalmedia.tv/acquisitions
records.notes: Digital distributor placing films across iTunes, Amazon, Google Play, cable VOD. Accepts broad range including faith and family content. Source: freestyledigitalmedia.tv.

records.name: Monterey Media
records.submission_process: Via montereymediadist.com/contact
records.notes: US home entertainment and digital distributor with family and general catalogue. Source: montereymediadist.com.

records.name: Taskovski Films
scouts_festivals: Hot Docs Canadian International Documentary Festival
records.submission_process: Via taskovskifilms.com/submissions
records.notes: European documentary sales agency with international reach. Actively acquires at IDFA and Sheffield. Source: taskovskifilms.com.

records.name: Journeyman Pictures
records.submission_process: Via journeyman.tv/about/submit
records.notes: UK-based international documentary distributor and broadcaster. Source: journeyman.tv.

records.name: Shorts International
records.submission_process: Via shortsinternational.com/submit
records.notes: World's leading short film distributor. Distributes across iTunes, Amazon, airlines, and broadcast in 100+ territories. Directly relevant for short film matching. Source: shortsinternational.com.

records.name: Seed&Spark
records.submission_process: Via seedandspark.com/distribute
records.notes: US crowdfunding and streaming platform with explicit commitment to underrepresented filmmakers. Source: seedandspark.com.

records.name: levelFILM
scouts_festivals: Hot Docs Canadian International Documentary Festival
records.submission_process: Via levelfilm.com/acquisitions
records.notes: Canada's leading independent film distributor. Source: levelfilm.com.

records.name: Mongrel Media
scouts_festivals: Hot Docs Canadian International Documentary Festival
records.submission_process: Via mongrelmedia.com/contact or festival acquisition
records.notes: Major Canadian distributor for international and Canadian cinema. Source: mongrelmedia.com.

records.name: Madman Entertainment
records.submission_process: Via madman.com.au/acquisitions
records.notes: Australia and New Zealand's leading independent distributor. Source: madman.com.au.

records.name: Palace Films
records.submission_process: Via sales agent; operates Palace cinema chain providing theatrical platform
records.notes: Australian arthouse theatrical distributor with own cinema chain. Source: palacefilms.com.au.

records.name: MAD Solutions
records.submission_process: Verify current submission route on official site — own-site verification pending
records.notes: Leading MENA-region distributor specialising in Arab cinema and regional international titles. Record built from trade coverage only — own-site verification still pending. Treat as provisional until confirmed.

records.name: Silverbird Film Distribution
records.submission_process: Verify current submission route on official site — own-site verification pending
records.notes: Named in current Nollywood trade coverage (2026) as a market-leading Nigerian theatrical distributor. Record built from trade coverage only — own-site verification still pending. Treat as provisional until confirmed.

records.name: ARRAY Releasing
records.submission_process: Mission-driven acquisitions; verify current submission route on ARRAY's own site
records.notes: Founded by Ava DuVernay in 2011 (as AFFRM, rebranded ARRAY 2015) as a distribution arm dedicated to amplifying films by Black artists and, per its stated mission, filmmakers of colour and women. Distributed the Netflix documentary 13th.

records.name: FilmOne Entertainment
records.submission_process: Direct distribution deals and co-productions; approach via its own site
records.notes: Per its own site: the largest theatrical distributor in West Africa (75% market share, 2022), the largest supplier of African content to Netflix under a multi-year deal since 2017, and exclusive theatrical licensee for Disney, Warner Bros. Discovery and Sony in Anglophone West Africa. Released the top 10 Nollywood titles of all time. Also holds an output deal with Angel Studios for faith titles in Anglophone West Africa (The Hollywood Reporter, 2025). Directly relevant to Lagos-anchored productions like the EKO VIBES reference case.

records.name: Wolfe Video
scouts_festivals: Frameline (San Francisco International LGBTQ+ Film Festival)
records.submission_process: Acquisitions team scouts named festivals and markets (its own published statement); also reviews submissions
records.notes: The longest-running and largest exclusive distributor of LGBTQ+ films (founded 1985), releasing across streaming, theatrical, festivals and international sales. Its own site names the festivals its acquisitions team scouts each year — one of the strongest verified distributor-festival linkages in this database.

records.name: Well Go USA Entertainment
records.submission_process: Acquisitions-led; known for festival and market pickups (e.g. Adelaide Film Festival pickup Monolith)
records.notes: Independent US distributor and a major home for Asian action and martial-arts cinema (Ip Man series, Burning), expanding across horror and indie titles; releases theatrically (often via AMC) and on VOD.

records.name: Angel Studios
records.submission_process: Distinctive model: crowd-vote greenlighting via Angel Guild membership and a regulated crowdfunding platform (Angel Funding)
records.notes: Faith-based and family-friendly distributor behind Sound of Freedom and King of Kings, positioned per The Hollywood Reporter as a family-friendly alternative to mainstream distributors, with global output deals including FilmOne for Anglophone West Africa. The database's first genuine faith-audience route.

records.name: Samuel Goldwyn Films
records.submission_process: IMPORTANT: explicitly does NOT accept unsolicited scripts or materials (its own site) — approach via sales agents/producers reps only
records.notes: Independent distributor of arthouse, independent and foreign features (Another Round, Lunana), with a documented track record in faith-audience hits (Facing the Giants, Fireproof per trade coverage) despite being a secular distributor.
